success_surface: Multi-sheet data comparison with conditional filtering and result population
effective_behaviors: Programmatically inspect available sheet names before accessing any worksheet
Use efficient data structures (sets) for cross-sheet value comparison and membership testing
Skip header rows explicitly when extracting data values from worksheets
Clear target range completely before writing new filtered results
Implement exception handling within filter logic for special-case values
Save modified workbook to output path without altering source file
Verify final output by reloading saved file and inspecting cell contents
evidence: Used wb.sheetnames to confirm sheet structure before accessing Source, Source2, Result
Built source_values and source2_values as sets for O(1) membership testing across sheets
Applied min_row=2 in iter_rows() calls to skip header rows during data extraction
Cleared existing Result sheet data with loop setting cells to None before writing new values
Added conditional logic for value 2: include if present in both sources despite other exclusion rules
Saved to new output_path instead of overwriting original 1_384-4_init.xlsx
Re-loaded saved file as wb_verify and printed Result sheet content to confirm correct values

memory_items.title: Cross-Sheet Set Operations
memory_items.lesson: When comparing values across multiple sheets, extract unique values into sets for efficient membership testing and filtering operations.
memory_items.generalization: For multi-source data comparisons, load values into Python sets first, then apply logical conditions (intersection, difference, exception rules) before writing results.

memory_items.title: Target Range Preparation
memory_items.lesson: Always clear existing content in the destination range before writing new filtered or calculated data to prevent stale entries or duplicates.
memory_items.generalization: Before populating a result area, verify and reset it to an empty state, preserving only structural elements like headers.

memory_items.title: Post-Execution Verification
memory_items.lesson: Reload the saved workbook and inspect the actual output values to confirm the manipulation produced expected results.
memory_items.generalization: After saving changes, perform a validation read-back on key result ranges to catch any unintended side effects before reporting completion.

memory_items.title: Header-Aware Data Extraction
memory_items.lesson: Explicitly skip header rows (typically row 1) when extracting data values for processing to avoid contaminating data with column labels.
memory_items.generalization: Configure row iteration parameters to start at min_row=2 when the first row contains metadata rather than data values.

edits.content:
## 6. Multi-Sheet Operations
- **Set-Based Comparisons**: When comparing values across multiple sheets, extract unique values into Python sets for efficient membership testing and filtering.
- **Exception Handling**: Implement conditional logic within filters to handle special-case values that require different inclusion criteria.
- **Destination Reset**: Always clear existing content in the target range before writing new filtered or calculated data to prevent duplicates.
- **Verification Loop**: After saving, reload the workbook and inspect key result ranges to confirm manipulation integrity.
- **Header Awareness**: Configure row iteration to skip header rows (min_row=2) when extracting data values for processing.
